name: Java - Spring MVC
scope: A person with BASIC proficiency level in Java - Spring MVC as a competency should be able to design and implement a small-to-medium monolithic web module using the classic DispatcherServlet stack, following controller-service-repository layering and delivering WAR or fat-JAR artefacts. They write solid Java SE code applying OOP, generics, streams, lambdas, try-with-resources and use core libraries (java.util, java.time, java.io, java.nio, java.net, java.lang.reflect). They understand JVM class-loading, memory areas, garbage collection basics and can diagnose ClassNotFoundException, OutOfMemoryError and checked-vs-unchecked exception flows. They configure Spring IoC through XML or @Configuration classes, employ component stereotypes (@Component/@Service/@Repository/@Controller), manage bean scopes (singleton, prototype, request, session) and lifecycle callbacks, leverage component scanning, qualifiers and PropertySources. They grasp the full request flow—handler mapping, adapter, view resolution—and create @RequestMapping/@GetMapping/@PostMapping methods that bind path-vars, query params, headers, cookies or multipart files via @ModelAttribute, @Valid and JSR-303/380 validators, returning Model/ModelMap/ModelAndView to JSP, JSTL, Thymeleaf or FreeMarker views while following the PRG pattern. They expose simple REST endpoints with @ResponseBody, MappingJackson2HttpMessageConverter and content negotiation, remain synchronous but know async exists, and implement global error handling via @ExceptionHandler and @ControllerAdvice. They integrate persistence through Spring JDBC Template or Spring Data JPA, configure pooled or embedded DataSource, annotate service methods with @Transactional, perform CRUD, map entities to DTOs (manual, ModelMapper, MapStruct) and run SQL/Flyway scripts for schema evolution. They externalize configuration in .properties or YAML, activate dev/test/prod profiles, employ SLF4J with Logback or Log4j2 (MDC aware) and can tweak or disable Spring Security auto-config when needed. They build with Maven or Gradle, manage dependency scopes, package as WAR for Tomcat/Jetty/Payara or run embedded servers via Spring Boot, and work comfortably in IntelliJ IDEA, Eclipse or VS Code while following Git branch/merge/rebase workflows. They write unit tests in JUnit 5, mock collaborators with Mockito, hit controller endpoints through MockMvc and the Spring TestContext Framework, enforce quality with SpotBugs/PMD/Checkstyle and JaCoCo, and profile hotspots with VisualVM or IDE profilers. They troubleshoot using application logs, Spring MVC debug traces, Postman/curl/browser DevTools, and recognise pool exhaustion, session stickiness, XSS and CSRF as common production risks. Finally, they participate in story estimation, code reviews, naming-standard adherence and concise Markdown/JavaDoc documentation to contribute smoothly within a team.
